title: STANDARD FOR MILK POWDERS AND CREAM POWDER CXS 207-1999

sections.section: STANDARD FOR MILK POWDERS AND CREAM POWDER CXS 207-1999
sections.summary: Document title: Standard for Milk Powders and Cream Powder (CXS 207-1999).
Adoption and amendment history: standard adopted in 1999 and subsequently amended in 2010, 2013, 2014, 2016, 2018, 2022 and 2023.
Notes 2022 amendments: following the 2022 Codex Alimentarius Commission decisions, Section 7.5 on labelling of non‑retail containers was amended.
Notes 2023 amendments: food additive provisions were amended and aligned with the General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, CXS 192‑1995) as part of additive‑provisions alignment.

Permits only additive functional classes indicated as technologically justified for milk/cream powders per the referenced GSFA tables, specifically allowing certain acidity regulators, anticaking agents, antioxidants (per Tables 1–2) and selected emulsifiers, firming agents and stabilizers (per Table 3) for category 01.5.1 (milk powder and cream powder (plain)).

Requires products and the milk used to comply with maximum levels for contaminants and toxins in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193‑1995) and with Codex maximum residue limits for veterinary drugs and pesticides in milk.

Recommends preparing and handling products in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1‑1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57‑2004) and other relevant Codex texts, and complying with any microbiological criteria established per CXG 21‑1997.

Adds specific labelling provisions to general labelling and dairy‑term standards, covering the prescribed product names, conditions for declaring milkfat and protein, an ingredient‑listing exception for milk used only for protein adjustment, and non‑retail container labelling per CXS 346‑2021.

Specifies permitted product names (Cream powder, Whole milk powder, Partly skimmed/semi‑skimmed milk powder, Skimmed milk powder) and allows alternative national designations; partly skimmed may be called 'semi‑skimmed' only if milkfat is between 14% and 16% m/m, with composition referenced to Section 3.2.

Requires declaration of milkfat content where omission would mislead consumers, either as percentage by mass or as grams per serving (with the number of servings stated), in a manner acceptable in the country of sale.

Requires milk protein content to be declared when omission would mislead the consumer, either as percentage by mass or as grams per serving (with number of servings stated), in a manner acceptable in the country of sale to the final consumer.

Permits omission from the ingredients list of milk products used solely for protein adjustment (i.e., such milk products need not be declared), notwithstanding Section 4.2.1 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre‑packaged Foods (CXS 1‑1985).
States that labelling of non‑retail containers should follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non‑Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346‑2021).
Mandates using the methods of analysis and sampling in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234‑1999), as relevant to this standard's provisions, for checking compliance.
Informational note stating that the additional information that follows does not affect the preceding provisions that are essential to product identity, name use and food safety.

Specifies additional quality limits for whole, partially skimmed and skimmed milk powder: titratable acidity max 18.0 (ml 0.1 N NaOH/10 g solids‑not‑fat), scorched particles max Disc B, and solubility index max 1.0 ml, with test methods per CXS 234‑1999.
Bibliographic note listing referenced Codex standards and codes (e.g., CXS 192, CXS 193, CXC 1, CXC 57, CXG 21, CXS 1, CXS 206, CXS 346, CXS 234).
Non‑substantive appendix heading with no substantive content provided.
